methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Avalanche Energy is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Robin Langtry that has raised $80M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set of three same-stage, same-sector companies—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—supplemented by a broader comp universe of 8 private and 1 public company in the climate and energy space.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage step-ups, implies $160M. Peer-median analysis across the three identified comps yields $150M, with the peer range spanning $110M to $200M. Both methods are applicable and yield closely aligned results.

The triangulated valuation range is $128M (low), $155M (median), and $184M (high). The tight clustering around the median suggests consistency across approaches. Primary uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set given heterogeneity in capital deployment models across frontier-energy companies, and the relevance of public-market comparables to early-stage private valuations. Stage progression relies on normalized multipliers that may not account for company-specific risk or market conditions specific to the target's fundraising environment.
